summary: Patrick McKenzie reports his best month yet for Bingo Card Creator, with $553.70 in profit from 28 sales on roughly 6 hours of work. His purchase page redesign successfully drove CD sales, and Google Checkout is saving money on fulfillment costs. However, Google AdWords is performing terribly with only 1 sale attributable to $90 in spend, giving it a heavily negative ROI. He's seeing strong organic growth with downloads hitting 1,100 (up from 200 in September), though visitor-to-download conversion rates are falling as less qualified traffic increases. He outlines priorities for February including fixing AdWords ROI, redesigning the download page, and finally adopting source control.
key_points: Revenue hit $698.65 gross on 28 sales with $553.70 profit, tracking toward his $1,000/month goal by April, all on roughly 6 hours of work
Google AdWords generated only 1 sale against $90 in spend, making it a clear money-loser that needs to be fixed or cut entirely
Organic search traffic is growing rapidly but converting at lower rates (12%) compared to highly qualified CPC traffic (25%), dragging overall conversion down to 16%
Downloads grew from 200 in September to 1,100 in January, showing strong compounding growth driven by improving search rankings
He's still not using source control and nearly lost work trying to revert a Mac build, prompting him to finally prioritize adopting it
money_quotes: Yes, Virginia, that IS equivalent to taking $65 out my my wallet and setting fire to it.
No reason to pay Google a tax just for the privilege of paying them a tax.
AdWords needs to have positive ROI or it needs to get cut, it is as simple as that.
Hard to believe that back in September I was ecstatic when this number hit 200.
No less than a quarter of my paying customer base is checking for a new version at least monthly. This gives me a warm fuzzy feeling inside.
I nearly had a heart attack trying to revert to a Mac version that was known to be good.
Need to find a new method of attack to get some more sales growth rather than just resting on my laurels, which candidly is a fairly accurate description of my marketing for 3 months now.
key_insight: Even with strong organic growth compounding month over month, every marketing channel must justify its ROI or be cut — paying for traffic that doesn't convert is just burning money.
